name: Mirador del meandro del río Jándula - loop from Andújar
name_original: Mirador del meandro del río Jándula - bucle desde Andújar
region_intro: Hiking around Andújar offers exploration within the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park, characterized by the gentle undulations of the Sierra Morena. The landscape features extensive Mediterranean forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, and gall oaks, alongside riverine vegetation along the Jándula and Yeguas rivers. Altitudes range from 400 to 1,290 meters, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Key water features include the Jándula, Encinarejo, and Rumblar reservoirs. Best hiking trails around Andújar The most popular hiking route is Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza - circular through the Sierra de Andújar natural park, an 11.1 miles (17.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 9 minutes to complete. This circular route traverses the natural park, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador del meandro del río Jándula - loop from Andújar, a difficult 13.9 miles (22.4 km) path. This route provides views of the Jándula river meander and surrounding landscapes. Local hikers also love the Encinarejo Dam loop from Los Llanos, a 3.8 miles (6.2 km) trail leading through areas near the Encinarejo Dam, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Hiking in Andújar is defined by Mediterranean forests, river valleys, and reservoir views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Andújar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Andújar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Andújar?
A: Andújar offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 30 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to different fitness levels, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Andújar?
A: Hiking in Andújar takes you through the gentle undulations of the Sierra Morena, characterized by extensive Mediterranean forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, and gall oaks. You'll also find riverine vegetation along the Jándula and Yeguas rivers, as well as scenic views of reservoirs like the Jándula, Encinarejo, and Rumblar.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Andújar?
A: The Sierra de Andújar Natural Park is enjoyable year-round, but spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Winter can also be a good time for hiking, especially for wildlife spotting, though some higher areas might be cooler.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Andújar?
A: Yes, Andújar has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17265976" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinarejo Dam loop from Los Llanos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.8-mile (6.2 km) route that takes about 1 hour 37 minutes to complete, offering pleasant views near the dam. There are 13 easy routes in total.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Andújar?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Andújar offers several difficult routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984057382/mirador-del-meandro-del-rio-jandula-loop-from-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del meandro del río Jándula - loop from Andújar&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 13.9-mile (22.4 km) path that provides expansive views of the Jándula river meander. There are 5 difficult routes in the area.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Andújar?
A: Yes, many of the hiking trails in Andújar are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981146799/santuario-virgen-de-la-cabeza-circular-through-the-sierra-de-andujar-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza - circular through the Sierra de Andújar natural park&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.1-mile (17.8 km) trail that offers varied scenery within the natural park.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant water features to see on hikes in Andújar?
A: Yes, the region around Andújar features beautiful water elements. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lastras waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The Jándula, Encinarejo, and Rumblar reservoirs also provide scenic views and opportunities for low-impact water activities.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails in Sierra de Andújar Natural Park?
A: The Sierra de Andújar Natural Park is a sanctuary for diverse wildlife. It's one of the last refuges for the endangered Iberian lynx. Hikers might also spot wolves, imperial eagles, golden eagles, black vultures, wild cats, deer, and wild boar. Birdwatching is particularly rewarding, with 178 different bird species identified.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible via hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. A significant cultural landmark is the Sanctuary of the Virgen de la Cabeza, perched on the Cerro del Cabezo. This historic site is the destination of Spain's oldest pilgrimage and offers spectacular views. You can explore it via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981146799/santuario-virgen-de-la-cabeza-circular-through-the-sierra-de-andujar-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza - circular through the Sierra de Andújar natural park&lt;/a&gt;. The area also has archaeological sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3069525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Batanera Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3169663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaescrita Cave Paintings&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there viewpoints along the hiking trails in Andújar?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park is dotted with viewpoints offering panoramic vistas. Notable spots include Mirador del Monumento al Peregrino, El Tamujar, Migorramos, and Valmayor River Viewpoint. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4363470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polar Viewpoint – Aldea del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent spot to take in the scenery.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Andújar?
A: The hiking routes in Andújar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the tranquility of the Mediterranean forests, and the stunning views of the river valleys and reservoirs.

Q: Is Andújar a good destination for astrotourism?
A: Yes, the sky above the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park has been declared a Starlight Reserve, making it an ideal location for astrotourism and stargazing after your daytime hikes.
tips: Nice viewpoint with a panoramic perspective of a meander of the Jándula river that makes it a spectacular point to photograph it.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of the Río Jándula Meander
